prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly black frame; only a small on-screen graphic or watermark is faintly visible in the lower-right corner.

It then cuts to a close-up of a white spray bottle being held or moved in front of a bright yellow-green background. The bottle label is partly visible and reads “fantastik,” with a blue label area and colored design elements.

Next, the scene shows a white tiled surface, likely a kitchen counter or wall area, with brownish-yellow greasy-looking spills or stains spread across the grout and tile. In the following frame, the same tiled surface appears clean, while a blurred yellow object or motion passes along the right side of the image, suggesting wiping or cleaning action, though the exact object is not clearly visible.

The commercial then shows a woman in a bright yellow top standing in a bright kitchen-like space. She has voluminous curly hair and is holding the spray bottle, looking down at it. A window and pale interior surfaces are visible behind her.

The final frames present a product shot: a white Fantastik trigger-spray bottle standing on a tiled surface, with a softly blurred kitchen background. Large black text appears on the left reading:

“No Grease.  
No Film.  
New Fantastik.”

Small copyright text is visible near the lower-left area, and a small watermark remains in the lower-right corner. In the last frame, colorful short brushstroke-like marks radiate around the product bottle, emphasizing it visually while the same text remains on screen.
